hero.title: Memory that keeps time.
hero.subtitle: INITE Brain is an open-source, per-tenant memory layer for AI agents. Bitemporal facts, hybrid retrieval, conflict-aware ingest, and a GDPR forget that actually works — over REST and a native MCP endpoint. Self-host it, or use the managed cloud.

panel.title: Bitemporal readout
panel.note: Every fact carries two clocks. Query 'now' by default; pass asOf to replay exactly what Brain knew at any past moment.

selfHost.label: Open source
selfHost.title: Self-host
selfHost.desc: Clone, configure, and run the whole stack on your own infra. AGPL-3.0 — yours to audit, fork, and extend.
bullets: Docker Compose — SurrealDB + service in minutes
Local BGE-M3 embedder, zero external embedding calls
Full admin cockpit + eval suite included

managed.label: Managed
managed.title: brain.inite.ai
managed.desc: Skip the ops. A hosted, multi-tenant endpoint with the same API, auto-deployed on every push to main.
bullets: Same REST + MCP surface as self-host

stats.title: Production gates, not vanity numbers.
stats.subtitle: Every push runs the multi-vertical retrieval + memory-lifecycle eval. The bars below are CI floors — drop under and a release blocks.
stats.footnote: Numbers from the multi-vertical scenario suite (n=255) + 180 wikidata queries. Bootstrap-CI on every retrieval metric.

items.title: Bitemporal facts
items.desc: Two axes per fact — valid time (real world) and transaction time (what brain knew when). Default queries answer 'actual now'; pass asOf for historical investigations.

items.title: Content predicates (v1.1)
items.desc: First-class vocabulary for content-domain knowledge — comments, reviews, tags, brands, categories. Each predicate carries semantics, decay, and PII class up front.

items.title: Lifecycle correctness = 1.0
items.desc: Update, supersede, retract, forget. The eval gate refuses anything under 1.0. GDPR forget is synchronous and leaves only an HMAC tombstone.

items.title: Conflict resolution
items.desc: Two ingests for the same fact go through a scored ladder (confidence × source-trust × recency × authority). Margins matter — close calls land as COMPETING, not silent overwrites.

items.title: MCP, native
items.desc: Per-tenant Streamable HTTP endpoint with six scope-aware tools. Claude Desktop, Cursor, Goose, n8n — same URL, same auth header, no glue code.

items.title: Hybrid retrieval
items.desc: Vector + BM25 + optional HyPE + cross-encoder + listwise LLM rerank with self-consistency. Every stage is a feature flag with per-tenant metrics.

quickstart.title: Three ways to talk to brain.
quickstart.subtitle: Pick the one that matches your runtime.

mcpBlock.title: Plug a Claude into your knowledge graph in one line.
mcpBlock.subtitle: Per-tenant MCP URL, scope-aware. The same six tools across every client.
mcpBlock.clients: Claude Desktop, Cursor, Goose, n8n — same URL, same auth header, no glue code.

skillsBlock.title: Skills for the agent, not just the wire.
skillsBlock.subtitle: Four Anthropic-format skills that teach the agent how to use brain — search, recall, bitemporal, and the setup walkthrough itself.

openSource.title: Built in the open, licensed AGPL-3.0.
openSource.subtitle: Read the code, run your own, send a PR. Everything that runs brain.inite.ai lives in the repo.

license.note: Network copyleft — self-host freely; changes you serve over a network stay open.

cta.title: Star the repo
cta.desc: Follow development, file issues, and help shape the roadmap.

footer.tagline: Open-source bitemporal knowledge graph for AI agents. A system of insight, not a system of record.

docs.indexSubtitle: Concepts, REST API, MCP setup, operator runbook.

getting-started.title: Getting started
getting-started.description: Auth, your first ingest, your first search. ~5 minutes.

bitemporal.title: Bitemporal model
bitemporal.description: Two time axes per fact. asOf semantics with Allen-interval examples.

predicates.title: Predicate vocabulary
predicates.description: Single-active vs bitemporal vs append-only. Decay, PII class, v1.1 content predicates.

conflict-resolution.title: Conflict resolution
conflict-resolution.description: Scoring formula, COMPETING / SUPERSEDED / REJECTED outcomes, dead-letter.

search.title: POST /v1/search
search.description: Hybrid retrieval pipeline, request / response shapes, per-leg CI.

synthesize.title: POST /v1/synthesize
synthesize.description: Corrective-RAG with strict / lenient / off guardrails + claim-level faithfulness scorer.

ingest.title: POST /v1/ingest/*
ingest.description: Structured fact ingest, NLU mention extraction, typed edges.

multi-hop.title: POST /v1/search/multi-hop
multi-hop.description: Planner LLM decomposes the query, joint-F1 evaluable supporting facts.

retract.title: POST /v1/facts/:id/retract
retract.description: Mark a fact retracted. Survives in audit trail with reason.

mcp-setup.title: MCP setup
mcp-setup.description: Claude Desktop, Cursor, Goose, n8n configs.

mcp-tools.title: MCP tools reference
mcp-tools.description: Six scope-aware tools with their full Zod schemas.

skills.title: Skills bundle
skills.description: Four Anthropic-format skills, install.sh, versioning model.

operator-playbook.title: Operator playbook
operator-playbook.description: Day-2 ops — keys, scopes, dreams cron, baseline diffs.

security.title: Security model
security.description: JWT, scopes, per-tenant isolation, GDPR forget, PII gating.

leases.title: Leases & claims
leases.subtitle: Leader-election + active job_run claims across the fleet. Use this to spot stuck workers, dead leases that haven't been re-acquired, or pods holding more than their share.

queueModeTip.enqueue: crons enqueue rows; worker loop dispatches
queueModeTip.inline: kill switch active — crons run inline (pre-Phase-J behaviour)

leases.poolTip: JobWorkerPool — node:worker_threads pool for cpuBound handlers (currently no handler opts in; size=0 disables the pool)

leaderLeases.title: Leader leases
leaderLeases.subtitle: Named locks in leader_lease (system DB). Multi-pod deploys elect one holder per name.
leaderLeases.empty: No leases held. Either the system is fresh or every cron has released its lease cleanly.

activeClaims.title: Active claims
activeClaims.subtitle: job_run rows currently in 'running' status. Stale heartbeats are reaped by LeaseManagerService.
activeClaims.empty: No active claims. The queue is empty or every worker just finished a cycle.

jobs.title: Job runs
jobs.subtitle: Long-running operator jobs (dreams / compaction / calibration refit / reindex / changefeed drain). SSE-streamed transitions; per-job drill shows progress / result / error.
jobs.sseHint: live SSE on accepted job_run transitions

maintenance.title: Maintenance cockpit
maintenance.subtitle: Scheduled jobs + manual triggers. Each card shows the last persisted run (via job_run + SchedulerRegistry), next-fire ETA, and a Run now button that delegates to the same code path the cron uses.

compaction.title: Compaction (hot → warm tier)
compaction.description: 03:17 UTC — facts past retention move to warm tier, optional LLM summary.

calibration_refit.title: Calibration refit (isotonic)
calibration_refit.description: 03:51 UTC — refit isotonic map from last-30d retraction outcomes. The same trigger also kicks source-trust refit.

source_trust_refit.title: Source-trust refit
source_trust_refit.description: 03:42 UTC — upserts source_trust(rate, sampleCount). Bundled with the calibration-refit trigger above.

reindex_embeddings.title: Reindex embeddings
reindex_embeddings.description: No cron — re-embed knowledge_fact across all tenants. Run after EMBEDDER_PROVIDER swap or model dimension change.

changefeed_drain.title: Changefeed drain → audit_event
changefeed_drain.description: Every minute — SHOW CHANGES per source, write audit_event, advance cursor.

maintenance.noRecentJobs: No persisted job runs yet. Trigger one above or wait for the next cron tick.
